jokes.text: That was... the second most awkward way a man has ever grabbed my breast.
jokes.setup: A man (Paul) apparently just grabbed Leslie's breast inappropriately during what seems like a ceremony
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact ranking of awkward breast-grabbing incidents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - instead of outrage or shock, Leslie treats it like a ranking system. Perfect economy and character-specific deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: We only have one chance to make a second impression.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to motivate the team about their next project after Harvest Festival success
jokes.punchline: Malapropism mixing 'first impression' with 'second chance'
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie malapropism that still makes logical sense in her world. Good character integration but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: What portion of this camping trip will take place outside? - All of it. - Pass.
jokes.setup: Tom asking about outdoor portions of camping trip
jokes.punchline: Immediate rejection when told it's entirely outside
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - his hatred of nature delivered with crisp setup/punchline structure.

jokes.text: What if we're scaling a cliff and I start to fall? Can I grab onto your boob for support?
jokes.setup: Tom asking a hypothetical camping safety question
jokes.punchline: The question is really about grabbing Leslie's breast
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to the opening incident, though the setup is a bit forced.

jokes.text: No, Tom, don't! Her boob kills!
jokes.setup: Tom's question about grabbing Leslie's breast for support
jokes.punchline: Jerry treating Leslie's breast like a weapon
jokes.explanation: Jerry's earnest warning treats the absurd premise seriously, escalating the running gag effectively.

jokes.text: My boob does not kill. Paul did not die. He had a heart attack which required an octuple bypass.
jokes.setup: Jerry claiming her boob kills
jokes.punchline: Leslie's defense accidentally reveals Paul nearly died
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - earnestly defending herself while providing evidence that supports the opposite conclusion.

jokes.text: Of course, I am gonna miss Indianapolis, which is the most wonderful city in America.
jokes.setup: Chris just said Pawnee is literally the greatest town in the country
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by calling Indianapolis the most wonderful city
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's tendency to be overly positive about everything, creating logical contradictions.

jokes.text: You're all my little hydration packs.
jokes.setup: Chris comparing Leslie's project to a hydration pack that gets him to the finish line
jokes.punchline: Extending the metaphor to call everyone hydration packs
jokes.explanation: Chris's fitness obsession manifests in weird workplace metaphors. Absurd but character-consistent.

jokes.text: I'm sure I am not. - Ron! You are too. Hydration pack!
jokes.setup: Chris calling everyone hydration packs
jokes.punchline: Ron's flat rejection followed by Chris's enthusiastic insistence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron vs Chris dynamic - Ron's matter-of-fact resistance to Chris's manic energy.

jokes.text: My identity was stolen. - His identity was stolen! Tell her about the accounts. - They were frozen. - Frozen.
jokes.setup: Andy excitedly asking Kyle to tell a 'hilarious' story
jokes.punchline: The story is just mundane identity theft with Andy providing unnecessary commentary
jokes.explanation: Andy's inability to recognize what makes a story interesting, treating serious problems as entertainment.

jokes.text: Camping sucks. It's super-boring. And you can see the stars, which I hate. They're creepy.
jokes.setup: April explaining why she doesn't want to go camping
jokes.punchline: Describing stars as creepy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly April - finding beauty in nature creepy. The escalation from boring to actively disturbing is great.

jokes.text: If you hate it, we'll leave and go make out. But if you love it, we'll stay and make out. Either way, a ton of making out.
jokes.setup: Andy proposing a solution to April's camping reluctance
jokes.punchline: Both outcomes lead to the same result he wants
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Andy's single-minded focus. Win-win scenarios that are really just what he wants anyway.

jokes.text: Ketchup and mustard. - Ketchup and mustard, I just was... Oh! That is so delightful. I relish your wit.
jokes.setup: Ann nervously saying 'ketchup and mustard' instead of 'catch up'
jokes.punchline: Chris enthusiastically punning with 'relish'
jokes.explanation: Forced condiment puns. Chris's enthusiasm makes it work better but it's still pretty groan-worthy.

jokes.text: Well, I salsa... your face.
jokes.setup: Trying to continue the condiment pun chain
jokes.punchline: Completely failing to make a coherent pun
jokes.explanation: Ann's awkward attempt to be witty fails spectacularly, which is the actual joke. Cringe comedy.

jokes.text: He was an amazing dentist.
jokes.setup: Talking about an ex she still liked after breaking up
jokes.punchline: The reason she liked him was his dental skills
jokes.explanation: Ann's practical approach to romance - appreciating professional competence. Good economy.

jokes.text: Remember, this is the guy that was so upbeat and positive, when he broke up with you, you didn't even know it happened.
jokes.setup: Ann wondering if Chris wants to get back together
jokes.punchline: Chris was so positive that Ann missed being dumped
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character observation about Chris's toxic positivity. Perfectly captures his personality flaw in one line.

jokes.text: Fishing relaxes me. It's like yoga, except I still get to kill something.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he wants to fish
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to yoga but emphasizing the killing aspect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson line. Subverts peaceful yoga expectation with his bloodlust. Incredibly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: It's called the ground when it's outside.
jokes.setup: Jerry saying he'll sleep on the floor outside
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: April's deadpan pedantry. Perfect economy - maximum snark per word.

jokes.text: Skymall! Come check out my tent. I ordered a bunch of crap off Skymall.
jokes.setup: Tom's solution to camping discomfort
jokes.punchline: Bringing consumer culture to the wilderness via airline catalog
jokes.explanation: Tom's character perfectly - solving problems with unnecessary purchases. Skymall is a perfect detail.

jokes.text: This is actually a dog couch, but it's super comfortable.
jokes.setup: Tom showing off his fancy camping setup
jokes.punchline: His 'awesome bed' is literally dog furniture
jokes.explanation: Tom's consumer obsession leads him to buy anything that seems fancy, even pet furniture.

jokes.text: About 83 cents. Per household. Before postage.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggesting returning Harvest Festival profits to citizens
jokes.punchline: The pathetically small amount gets even smaller
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how Jerry's 'generous' idea becomes absurd when examined. Ben's accounting precision makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, I know this one. They are all rap-pists. - Oh, my God, they're Rappers, Jerry!
jokes.setup: Tom asking what Jay-Z, Lil Wayne, and Drake have in common
jokes.punchline: Jerry mispronouncing 'rappers' as something inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry mistake - innocent mispronunciation creates awkwardness. Relies on sound similarity for humor.

jokes.text: Luxury Dog Park. Do you wanna tell us anymore? - Poodles only. No pooping.
jokes.setup: April presenting her minimal project idea
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary restrictions that defeat the purpose of a dog park
jokes.explanation: April's anti-participation manifests as deliberately bad ideas. The contradiction is perfect - a dog park with no pooping.

jokes.text: The air is too fresh. It's disgusting. I can't breathe. There's a brook somewhere that won't stop babbling. Shut up!
jokes.setup: April lost at the wrong campground
jokes.punchline: Treating natural beauty as personal annoyance
jokes.explanation: April's hatred of nature escalating to yelling at a brook. Perfect character consistency and escalation.

jokes.text: Great idea. Thanks, white Urkel.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting investing the money conservatively
jokes.punchline: April's dismissive nickname for Ben
jokes.explanation: April's casual cruelty disguised as observation. 'White Urkel' perfectly captures Ben's nerdy accountant energy.

jokes.text: That's what I'm calling my tent.
jokes.setup: Tom wanting to go back to 'the thunderdome'
jokes.punchline: He's nicknamed his camping tent after a post-apocalyptic arena
jokes.explanation: Tom's tendency to over-dramatize everything, even basic camping gear. Good economy.

jokes.text: Because I don't have one. I don't have an idea, Ann.
jokes.setup: Leslie's big speech about disappointing people and needing real ideas
jokes.punchline: She admits she has no idea after all that pressure
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Leslie's perfectionist facade. The vulnerability makes it work rather than just being a plot point.

jokes.text: I married Alf, and we're pretty happy.
jokes.setup: Leslie reading from her dream journal for inspiration
jokes.punchline: Mundane domestic happiness with an alien puppet
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist detail. The casual 'pretty happy' makes it funnier than if it were more dramatic.

jokes.text: You know, I was 24 when Gayle took my virginity.
jokes.setup: Discussion about teenagers and sexual activity
jokes.punchline: Jerry oversharing about losing his virginity very late
jokes.explanation: Jerry's tendency to overshare inappropriate details. The late age adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: What's say we just stand here in silence. And think of ideas... for projects.
jokes.setup: Jerry's uncomfortable oversharing
jokes.punchline: Ben's desperate attempt to change the subject
jokes.explanation: Ben's straight-man reaction to Jerry's awkwardness. The forced subject change is relatable.

jokes.text: You know what I should do? I should lead a teen abstinence workshop. That might be incredibly effective.
jokes.setup: Jerry just revealed he was a virgin until 24
jokes.punchline: He thinks this qualifies him to teach teenagers about abstinence
jokes.explanation: Jerry completely missing the irony that his late start makes him a cautionary tale, not a success story.

jokes.text: That's a good stopping point.
jokes.setup: Tom apparently getting a head massage in the woods
jokes.punchline: Treating wilderness camping like a spa experience
jokes.explanation: Tom bringing luxury services to camping. The casual interruption of relaxation for work is funny.

jokes.text: I just return it the next day and claim it was defective. The key is crying a lot. No one likes to hear a grown man cry.
jokes.setup: How Tom affords all his expensive camping gear
jokes.punchline: Elaborate return fraud involving fake crying
jokes.explanation: Tom's consumer lifestyle explained through petty fraud. The crying strategy is both pathetic and clever.

jokes.text: And then my hand accidentally went in the panini press!
jokes.setup: Tom demonstrating his return fraud crying technique
jokes.punchline: Fake injury story involving kitchen equipment
jokes.explanation: Tom's elaborate lies for minor fraud. Panini press is a funny specific detail.

jokes.text: It's pollution from the sweetums factory. - It's gorgeous. - But is it worth the asthma? - No.
jokes.setup: Andy commenting on the beautiful sky
jokes.punchline: April explaining it's actually pollution, Andy still finding it pretty, then changing his mind about the health effects
jokes.explanation: Good April/Andy dynamic - she's cynical but factual, he's simple but adaptive. Nice quick reversal.

jokes.text: I mean, you're like the energizer bunny of city government.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to compliment Leslie's work ethic
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to a battery-powered toy rabbit
jokes.explanation: Decent character observation but relies on familiar metaphor. Ben's awkward attempt at motivation.

jokes.text: I was just out for my nighttime run, and I thought, 'why not go visit the Parks Department?'
jokes.setup: Chris suddenly appearing at the remote campsite
jokes.punchline: Acting like randomly running to a campground is normal behavior
jokes.explanation: Chris's fitness obsession making him appear anywhere. His casual tone about the absurd distance is funny.

jokes.text: I'm much more receptive to new ideas when my heart rate is still elevated.
jokes.setup: Chris wanting to hear their idea immediately
jokes.punchline: Believing exercise affects his idea reception
jokes.explanation: Chris's pseudo-scientific approach to everything. Treating idea reception like athletic performance.

jokes.text: Los Angeles, season one, isn't gonna watch itself.
jokes.setup: Everyone wanting to leave the campsite
jokes.punchline: Tom's priority is catching up on reality TV
jokes.explanation: Tom's consumer culture priorities. The specific show reference makes it more real.

jokes.text: And that'll be fun. We'll all stay up, and then I'll be like, 'hey! No one sleep!' Do you hear how loud that is? You're not sleeping through that.
jokes.setup: Leslie declaring they'll pull an all-nighter
jokes.punchline: Her manic explanation of how she'll prevent people from sleeping
jokes.explanation: Leslie's control freak tendencies manifesting as manic energy. The escalation into shouting is good.

jokes.text: Jerry scared all the fish away with his loud personal stories.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why there's no food
jokes.punchline: Jerry's oversharing literally scaring wildlife
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that takes Jerry's character flaw and makes it have real consequences. Great economy.

jokes.text: I miss my canopy bed.
jokes.setup: Everyone complaining about camping conditions
jokes.punchline: Tom's specific luxury bed type
jokes.explanation: Tom's luxury lifestyle details. Canopy bed is pretentious enough to be funny.

jokes.text: Especially since my stupid boyfriend abandoned me!
jokes.setup: April wanting to leave camping
jokes.punchline: Blaming Andy who's been trying to find her all episode
jokes.explanation: April's unfair blame when the audience knows Andy's been searching for her. Good dramatic irony.

jokes.text: You know, I've never moved this slowly before. It's almost like being in quicksand.
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann walking away to talk privately
jokes.punchline: Chris treating normal walking pace like a physical challenge
jokes.explanation: Chris's fitness obsession making normal activities feel foreign. The quicksand comparison is dramatic.

jokes.text: Sounds good. - No, no.
jokes.setup: Chris asking Ann to dinner to 'talk about us'
jokes.punchline: Ann agreeing then immediately being warned off by Ben
jokes.explanation: Ben immediately recognizing Ann's mistake. Good timing but simple reaction beat.

jokes.text: Chocolate or butterscotch? - Uh, swirl me.
jokes.setup: Tom having fondue at the campsite
jokes.punchline: Jerry's enthusiastic participation in Tom's luxury camping
jokes.explanation: Jerry's excitement about fancy camping amenities. 'Swirl me' suggests he's really into it.

jokes.text: No! I was Tivoing Cupcake Wars.
jokes.setup: Tom's power being cut off for using too much electricity
jokes.punchline: His priority is missing reality TV shows
jokes.explanation: Tom's priorities being entertainment over outdoor experience. Cupcake Wars is a good specific detail.

jokes.text: What the Is a german muffin?
jokes.setup: Bed & breakfast owner listing breakfast options
jokes.punchline: April's confused reaction to made-up food item
jokes.explanation: April's directness cutting through pretentious B&B nonsense. Good reaction to absurd menu item.

jokes.text: This place is the exact opposite of Skymall.
jokes.setup: Tom hating the rustic bed & breakfast
jokes.punchline: Using his consumer paradise as the opposite benchmark
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Tom's consistent value system. Skymall as the ultimate good is funny.

jokes.text: This place is lamer than outside.
jokes.setup: April's hatred of the outdoors is well-established
jokes.punchline: Finding something even worse than nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect April line - taking her established hatred and finding something worse. Maximum economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: ♪ and I had to fight a squirrel ♪
jokes.setup: Andy singing about his adventure finding April
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of fighting woodland creatures
jokes.explanation: Andy's adventure getting absurd. Squirrel fighting fits his childlike approach to obstacles.

jokes.text: And I had to drink most of it to survive.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his romantic campsite setup
jokes.punchline: Drinking the champagne for 'survival' rather than romance
jokes.explanation: Andy reframing drinking as necessity rather than admitting he was nervous/bored. Good character logic.

jokes.text: Oh, no. - Oh, no? - Oh, no. - No, no, no. I wasn't saying that...
jokes.setup: Chris thinking Ann wants to get back together
jokes.punchline: Ann's mounting horror as she realizes the misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ann's realization. The repetition shows her panic building. Validates Leslie's earlier warning.

jokes.text: I'm gonna leave the country. Bye, everybody. Bye.
jokes.setup: Ann realizing she's in the same situation as before with Chris
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to flee internationally
jokes.explanation: Ann's pragmatic solution to an emotional problem - just leave entirely. The casual goodbyes are funny.

jokes.text: And actual cats, roughly 12 cats.
jokes.setup: Leslie complaining about her room having cat hair and smell
jokes.punchline: The reveal that there are actual living cats
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from cat evidence to actual cats. The specific number makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I am listening to Steal my Sunshine by Len. A one-hit wonder, like me.
jokes.setup: Leslie feeling like she peaked with Harvest Festival
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to an obscure 90s song
jokes.explanation: Great specific cultural reference that perfectly captures Leslie's self-pity. The song choice is perfect for the mood.

jokes.text: Remember that woman who came up with the Harvest Festival idea and never came up with another idea again? What happened to her? What was her name? Kim? Anyway, who cares? She's stupid, and she's dead now.
jokes.setup: Leslie imagining her future legacy
jokes.punchline: The imaginary people getting her name wrong and being dismissive
jokes.explanation: Leslie's perfectionist anxiety spiraling into absurd self-deprecation. The wrong name and casual cruelty are perfect details.

jokes.text: All due respect, Ms. Clack, stick a german muffin in it.
jokes.setup: B&B owner complaining about noise
jokes.punchline: Ron's polite but aggressive dismissal using her menu item
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron line - polite form with aggressive content. Using the absurd menu item as an insult is brilliant callback integration.

jokes.text: The transom is painted shut.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to escape through the window
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact explanation of her captivity
jokes.explanation: Ron's practical knowledge used for Leslie's imprisonment. His calm delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, there's, like, 50 cats in here.
jokes.setup: Leslie locked in the cat room overnight
jokes.punchline: The cat population has apparently grown
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the cat situation but pretty predictable. The growing number is a simple callback.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, my breath is so bad.
jokes.setup: Leslie just gave an inspiring speech about her new ideas
jokes.punchline: Immediately concerned about mundane personal hygiene
jokes.explanation: Good deflation after her triumphant moment. Leslie's attention to detail extending to personal appearance.

jokes.text: Yeah, she died, like, 20 minutes after that.
jokes.setup: Talking head about someone's happiness (unclear context)
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of immediate death after happiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy button. Jerry's matter-of-fact delivery of horrible news. Great economy and shocking content.
